candidates.title: UAD + DFDA with Dead Feature Analysis
candidates.summary: Validate UAD+DFDA on pretrained SAEs with varying dead feature ratios. Quantify how dead features confound absorption detection and confirm the pipeline generalizes to healthier dictionaries.
hypotheses: H1: Dead features reduce UAD precision but recall remains robust
H2: Collision rates converge across architectures when dead feature ratios are matched
H3: UAD achieves F1 >= 0.55 on pretrained SAEs with dead_feature_ratio < 50%
H4: DFDA achieves >=10% improvement on pretrained absorbed pairs
candidates.pilot_focus: UAD on 2-3 pretrained SAEs with known dead feature ratios; dead feature filtering ablation
key_risks: UAD F1 may drop below 0.5 on healthier dictionaries if precision was artificially inflated by dead feature sparsity
Pretrained SAEs may have different feature distributions than trained SAEs
Chanin labels may not be available for all pretrained SAEs
candidates.novelty_claim: First systematic quantification of dead feature confound on absorption detection; validation of unsupervised detection on healthy dictionaries

candidates.title: Co-Occurrence as General SAE Analysis Tool
candidates.summary: Generalize UAD's co-occurrence clustering beyond absorption to comprehensive unsupervised SAE analysis: hierarchy discovery, redundancy detection, community detection.
hypotheses: Co-occurrence clustering reveals semantic hierarchies without supervision
Network centrality metrics identify functionally important features
Community detection on co-occurrence graphs recovers semantic categories
candidates.pilot_focus: Validate co-occurrence clustering on known semantic categories across multiple pretrained SAEs
candidates.evidence_from_iter1: UAD clustering works on first-letter features; generalization to semantic hierarchies and healthier dictionaries untested
key_risks: Semantic hierarchies may not form clean clusters in co-occurrence space
Dead features may still confound community detection
candidates.novelty_claim: General-purpose co-occurrence analysis toolkit for SAEs
candidates.pivot_trigger: UAD F1 < 0.5 on pretrained SAEs but co-occurrence structure is still informative

candidates.title: Absorption as SAE Quality Diagnostic
candidates.summary: Treat absorption rate and dead feature ratio as joint diagnostic signals for SAE quality. Propose a composite 'SAE Health Index'.
hypotheses: Composite quality score (absorption * dead_feature_ratio) predicts interpretability better than either alone
Absorption rate negatively correlates with seed consistency
Dead feature ratio is a leading indicator of absorption susceptibility
candidates.pilot_focus: Collect 10+ pretrained SAEs, measure absorption vs quality metrics
candidates.evidence_from_iter1: Dead feature ratios were extremely high (94-99%) in trained SAEs; relationship to absorption untested across diverse SAEs
key_risks: Correlations may be weak or confounded by model architecture
Quality metrics themselves may be unreliable
candidates.novelty_claim: Reframes absorption from 'problem to fix' to 'signal to monitor'; first joint diagnostic framework
candidates.pivot_trigger: UAD fails but absorption/dead-feature relationship is still meaningful

candidates.title: Absorption as Consequence of SAE Nonidentifiability
candidates.summary: Challenge the field's framing by showing absorption patterns vary across SAEs with different dead feature ratios, suggesting absorption is an artifact of training configuration rather than a real feature property.
hypotheses: Absorption patterns vary significantly across SAEs for the same concept
Feature overlap across SAEs correlates negatively with absorption stability
The absorption classification is not reproducible across SAE configurations
candidates.pilot_focus: Compare absorption patterns across 3-5 pretrained SAEs for same model layer
candidates.evidence_from_iter1: EleutherAI found ~30% feature overlap across seeds; absorption stability across SAE configurations untested
key_risks: May be dismissed as 'gotcha' paper without constructive recommendations
If absorption IS stable across SAEs, hypothesis is falsified
candidates.novelty_claim: First systematic study of absorption stability across SAE configurations
candidates.pivot_trigger: UAD detects wildly different absorption patterns across SAEs with different dead feature ratios

pivot_criteria.if_uad_f1_below_0.5: Pivot to cand_b, cand_c, or cand_d based on observed failure mode
pivot_criteria.if_uad_f1_0.5_to_0.55: Proceed with cand_a but add cand_c as secondary contribution

synthesis_notes: Iteration 2 perspectives debated whether to fix dead features via AuxK training (innovator/pragmatist) or treat them as a confound to analyze (theoretical/contrarian). The synthesis rejects AuxK training because (1) it violates the project spec's training-free constraint, (2) it distracts from the already-validated UAD contribution, and (3) pretrained SAEs with varying dead feature ratios already provide sufficient variation for analysis. The front-runner maintains UAD+DFDA as core while incorporating dead feature insights as a confound analysis rather than a problem to fix.
